data.title: Treetop Companies buys 515,000 SF Pittsburgh Logistics Center for $32M, at $62 per sq ft, expanding its platform in the area.
data.description: Treetop Companies has recently acquired the Pittsburgh Logistics Center, a multi-tenant warehouse covering 515,411 square feet in Cheswick, Pennsylvania. The acquisition, costing $32 million, signifies a notable growth for Treetop's logistics presence in Pittsburgh, located close to the Alpha Logistics Center it acquired earlier. The transaction was valued at $62 per square foot, indicating a considerable discount compared to replacement expenses and recent market comparables.

data.title: Ohana Real Estate Investors sells ownership stake in Waldorf Astoria Monarch Beach Resort & Club to San Manuel Investment Authority.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, Ohana Real Estate Investors (Ohana) revealed the sale of their remaining ownership stake in the Waldorf Astoria Monarch Beach Resort & Club located in Dana Point, California. The luxury oceanfront resort consists of 400 keys. The sale was made to the San Manuel Investment Authority, which is associated with the Yuhaaviatam of San Manuel Nation. This significant deal showcases Ohana's proficiency in recognizing distinctive prospects and conducting value-adding strategies.
Zelenskyy: We need to sit and speak, we need ceasefire

data.title: Sanofi's Rezurock denied EU approval for cGVHD treatment. The CHMP rejected the drug on Friday. Sanofi plans re-examination.
data.description: The European Medicines Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) rejected Sanofi SA's application for approval of Rezurock (belumosudil) for treating chronic graft-versus-host disease (cGVHD) in adults and pediatric patients. cGVHD is a significant cause of morbidity and non-relapse mortality post stem cell transplant. Sanofi will request a re-evaluation of the CHMP decision. This news can be found in more detail on Benzinga.com.

data.title: UK health agency NICE recommends ViiV Healthcare's Apretude (cabotegravir) as injectable PrEP for high-risk HIV-1 individuals.
data.description: The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) in the U.K. suggests the use of Apretude (cabotegravir) by ViiV Healthcare, a company jointly owned by GSK Plc (NYSE: GSK), Pfizer Inc (NYSE: PFE), and Shionogi Inc, as a pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP) for individuals at high risk of HIV-1. This marks Apretude as the first injectable PrEP to be recommended by NICE. Apretude's recommendation is significant as it provides a new method of HIV prevention for those in need.
